channel.title: Comments for page "Awakenings, Part Two"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Awakenings, Part Two"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who is this Beezy mentioned in the first part?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I believe that "the bedroom where Donald's body lay" would be correct, perhaps the author wanted to keep the word "lie" in place because of the multiple definitions of that word?

That's probably something to reach out to the author to address, since… it really does look like it should be "lay". "Lie" even seems like the wrong verb tense anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looking at the edit history, I can see that the grammar edit I was going to make was made, and then reverted. While I believe that "the bedroom where Donald's body lay" would be correct, perhaps the author wanted to keep the word "lie" in place because of the multiple definitions of that word? Just curious.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit was reverted for falling outside of the range of allowable edits for a member who is not the author of this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll consider it, certainly. I'm already on shaky ground by doing a normal prose tale, so I wanted to be confident about the ideas in it. Maybe something can get edited in later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it quite a lot, though I of course admit to a bias.

For the record: Not every Awakening vignette needs to be an SCP, and while I understand not flat-out using the SCPs of other currently active authors, that doesn't mean you couldn't bring in one of the real old ones, or check with one of the currently active authors to see if they don't have any ideas for one of their SCPs that you've got your eye on…

…but you brought in so many neat ideas here with your own SCPs that I don't mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, yeah. I can't say I know anyone else's SCPs better than mine, so that's why I used mine. I'm kind of subpar at judging the nature of (or inventing a backstory for) SCPs I didn't write, so it's much more convenient for me to work with what I know.

Glad you liked it, though. I tried to imply that SCP-1606 was actually focused around her father, the house and Marissa being the only memories left that weren't devoured by the entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat ways of using a couple skips (though that they're all yours makes this kind of unsurprising), particularly the house. I'd always figured that Marissa Schmidt was the center of events there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just didn't find any of the parts particularly interesting. Sorry man.

item.title: New tale, bitches
item.content:encoded:
This is my first foray into another team's canon. As you might notice, all of these SCPs are mine. I apologize for nothing.

Edit: Some backstory that I like but haven't ever spelled out
Regarding some recurring characters and places

In the last vignette, in which Subject 1564-18 mentions that she was once called Jyoti Sanmugasunderam, it's not a coincidence that I reused the name from Misnomer — Jyoti is Satyana's younger sister who got recruited to D-class (the elder Sanmugasunderam was not informed of this, as the identities of all D-class are kept secret). It's up to you whether Coldest War and Competitive Dicksatology are co-canons or whether the same characters simply exist in multiple universes. SCP-1564's article, by the way, is set a ways in time after Misnomer (hence the elder Sanmugasunderam's receiving of a Doctorate), but about ten years before this tale. I have an explanation for why these people are interacting this way, but it's not particularly relevant to anything else that's happening. At least at the moment.
